name: Avatar Insta Split-Screen Reel
description: Instagram/Reels/Shorts split-screen format: b-roll on top (~53%) — a screen recording OR a tall article screenshot panned at a controllable scroll speed — and an AI avatar talking head on the bottom (~47%, auto-cropped from its content band), karaoke caption pills on the seam, a correctly-shaped hook badge, a low typing SFX bed over the first ~2s, and a click SFX on every b-roll cut. Localizes to the project language: English keeps the HeyGen text voice; non-English uses a dynamically-picked ElevenLabs voice (eleven_v3) lip-synced by the avatar, with localized captions, badge, and a platform-correct CTA. Built for promo/listicle shorts where a scrolling article (or screen recording) carries the proof while the avatar narrates.
best_for: 9:16 promo or explainer reels narrated by a HeyGen avatar
DFY listicle/'best X' reels: a published article scrolled under a talking head with a '#1 PICK' badge
non-English / localized reels (VO + captions + badge + CTA all follow the project language)
turning a product screen recording into beat-locked b-roll under a talking head
reusing one avatar render across many topics by swapping the b-roll + beat map
not_for: fullscreen talking-head with no screen recording (use captioned-talking-head)
fictional UGC creator paid ads (use ugc-ai-ad)
news/tutorial masters with source receipts and PiP (use avatar-explainer)

stages.title: Intake + script
actions: Collect the avatar id (CLI/HEYGEN_AVATAR_ID), the PROJECT LANGUAGE and target PLATFORM (YouTube vs Instagram), the b-roll source (a tall article screenshot for a scroll reel, or a screen recording), and the script.

Write the script IN THE PROJECT LANGUAGE (accents preserved; never em-dashes/smart-quotes/ellipsis). Hook -> listicle/mechanism beats -> CTA. Keep it tight; avatars speak slower than humans so a 33s human script lands near 40s. Whisper-verify brand-name pronunciation (name-safety).

stages.title: Generate avatar clip (language-aware)
actions: Run workflows/avatar-insta-split/gen_avatar.py --script-file script.txt --out avatar.mp4 --language <Lang> [--gender female] --avatar-id <ID> (9:16, normalized to project fps).

English -> HeyGen text voice (HEYGEN_VOICE_ID), unchanged. Non-English -> a dynamically-picked ElevenLabs voice (eleven_v3, tools/elevenlabs_voice.py) is uploaded to HeyGen and the avatar lip-syncs it via /v2/video/generate. Bundled model voice, NEVER a cloned personal voice.
HeyGen renders draw from a separate API credit pool; recover a timed-out poll by video_id instead of regenerating.

stages.title: B-roll + beat map
actions: Article-scroll reel (recommended): capture a tall article screenshot (capture_article.py <url> article.png — includes the headline at y=0 and writes section y-offsets), then author beats with per-beat scroll_from/scroll_to (px in the article at canvas width) for an explicit, tunable scroll SPEED. Include a HEADLINE beat (scroll_from:0).
Screen-recording reel (legacy): transcribe the avatar audio (Groq), split into beats on phrase breaks, assign each a broll_in + content-zoom crop CW:CH:CX:CY (aspect is now preserved — no squash).
Generate the hook badge in-pipeline (make_badge.py --brand <Brand> --language <Lang>) so it is correctly shaped + localized; set badge_png + badge_w. Write plan.json (copy plan.example.json).

stages.title: Build split-screen + captions + SFX
actions: It auto-crops the avatar content band into the bottom region, scrolls/cuts the b-roll per beat, renders karaoke pills on the seam (captions auto-follow the VO language via Whisper), overlays the correctly-scaled hook badge, mixes the typing bed + a click on each cut, and loudnorm-exports.
CTA: Instagram -> comment-DM (cta_word/cta_text). YouTube -> brand callout (cta_anchor:'tail', cta_text:'Zoek <Brand>', cta_word:null) — never 'comment'/'link in bio'.

stages.title: QC + export
actions: Sample a frame per beat: confirm no letterbox bars in the avatar region, captions on the seam, badge on the hook, relevant b-roll per beat.
Check loudness (~-16 LUFS), no clipping, no black frames, and that the typing bed is audible-but-low and clicks land on cuts.

providers.avatar: HeyGen (text voice for English; audio-driven lip-sync for non-English)
providers.voice_non_english: ElevenLabs eleven_v3 (dynamic voice pick from the shared library by language)

routing_keywords: screen recording on top talking head on bottom
comment to get the link reel
script_shape: Hook (one line, why they should care)
Mechanism beats (each beat = one b-roll surface that proves the claim)
Comment-gated CTA (held caption pill at the end)
qc_checks: headline (H1) is on screen during the opening beat (article-scroll reels)
the article pans at a readable speed and is NOT vertically squashed (aspect preserved)
the hook badge is correctly proportioned (not stretched) and shows only during its window
VO + captions + badge + CTA are all in the PROJECT LANGUAGE; brand name pronounces correctly
avatar region has no letterbox bars (content band detected)
caption pills sit on the seam and are time-aligned to the words
CTA matches the platform (Instagram comment-DM vs YouTube brand callout)
typing bed is low under the first ~2s; a click lands on each cut
loudness ~-16 LUFS, true peak <= -1.5 dBTP, no clipping, no black frames
compliance_notes: Only use an avatar of a real person with their permission or a licensed/own likeness.
Non-English VO uses a bundled ElevenLabs model voice — NEVER a cloned personal voice (likeness/brand risk at scale).
Language default is English: English reels are unchanged. Only non-English projects take the ElevenLabs path.
YouTube CTAs must be brand/search callouts (no 'comment'/'link in bio'); the comment-DM mechanic is Instagram-only.
Disclose AI presenters where the platform requires it (e.g. realistic AI content labels).
